 {"id":"bd-3o9b0","title":"Implement bead assignment integration","description":"Implement bead assignment integration with --bead flag.

## Feature
When --bead=<ID> is provided, automatically send bead assignment prompt after restart.

## Prompt Template
Read AGENTS.md, register with Agent Mail. Work on: <BEAD_ID> - <DESC>.
Use br show <BEAD_ID> for details. Use ultrathink.

## Implementation
1. Fetch bead info via br show --json
2. Format prompt with bead ID and description
3. Send prompt to pane after agent is ready
4. Return bead_assigned and prompt_sent in response

## Alternative: --prompt flag
Allow custom prompt instead of bead template for flexibility.

## Error Handling
- If bead ID doesn't exist: return error before restart
- If prompt send fails: note in response but still report restart success","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:25:25.108162363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:34:29.635186608Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T01:34:29.635053721Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3o9b0","depends_on_id":"bd-21tqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}

idempotency, jobs, and OpenAPI documentation.\n\n## Scope\n- API server scaffolding with chi router, middleware, error envelope, request IDs.\n- Core resources: system, sessions, panes, agents, output tooling, palette/history, checkpoints, metrics, context, git.\n- Tool bridges: Agent Mail + reservations, Beads/BV, CASS/CM, Scanner, Accounts, Safety, Pipelines.\n- OpenAPI 3.1 spec generation + Swagger UI at /docs.\n- Consistent envelopes + error codes aligned with robot mode.\n\n## Success Criteria\n- Every CLI/robot feature has a REST endpoint.\n- OpenAPI spec is generated from kernel and stays in sync.\n- Idempotent POSTs support Idempotency-Key.\n- Long‑running ops return job  {"id":"bd-3ol6","title":"E2E Tests: Full task assignment workflow","description":"E2E Tests for Full task assignment workflow.

## Test Scenarios

1. Basic assignment flow
   - Create test beads with bv
   - Spawn agents
   - Run ntm assign
   - Verify tasks distributed
   - Verify file reservations created

2. Priority-based assignment
   - Create P1, P2, P3 tasks
   - Run assignment
   - Verify P1 tasks assigned first
   - Verify P3 tasks queued

3. Dependency respect
   - Create tasks with dependencies
   - Run assignment
   - Verify blockers assigned before blocked

4. Interactive mode
   - Run ntm assign --interactive
   - Simulate user selections
   - Verify chosen assignments applied

## Detailed Logging
```
[E2E-ASSIGN] Loading tasks from bv: found N
[E2E-ASSIGN] Assigning TASK_ID to AGENT (priority=N)
[E2E-ASSIGN] Reserved files for AGENT: FILES
[E2E-ASSIGN] Assignment complete: N tasks to N agents
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 4 test scenarios implemented
2. Basic flow creates file reservations
3. P1 tasks assigned before P2/P3
4. Dependencies respected (blockers first)
5. Interactive mode responds to simulated input
6. Detailed logging with [E2E-ASSIGN] prefix
7. Exit code 0 on all pass, non-zero on any failure","acceptance_criteria":"1. E2E tests verify full task assignment
2. BV-driven assignment tested
3. Assignment persists correctly
4. Assignment notifications sent
5. Test cleanup removes assignments
6. Test duration under 5 minutes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:15:21.439072364Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T03:34:36.568982216Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T03:34:36.568475522Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ol6","depends_on_id":"bd-27ot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ol6","depends_on_id":"bd-9tbv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3pa8p","title":"Task: Unit tests for robot verbosity/short-keys/pagination","description":"## Overview
Add unit tests for robot output ergonomics features: verbosity profiles, short‑key mappings, and pagination/limits.

## Tests
- `--robot-verbosity=full|terse` toggles optional fields as specified
- Short‑key map produces stable, reversible keys (if enabled)
- Pagination returns deterministic `next_cursor` + correct `count`/`total` behavior
- Large payloads respect limits without breaking envelope

## Logging
Use `t.Logf` with structured prefixes:
- `t.Logf("ROBOT_TEST: verbosity=%s size=%d", mode, size)`
- `t.Logf("ROBOT_TEST: shortkeys=%t fields=%v", enabled, keys)`
- `t.Logf("ROBOT_TEST: page limit=%d next=%s", limit, next)`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic fixtures for each verbosity level
- Pagination behavior covered for edge cases (empty, exact limit, overflow)
- Tests run without external dependencies","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:24:13.235689238Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T03:56:02.061959482Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T03:56:02.061611244Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3pa8p","depends_on_id":"bd-20ong","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pa8p","depends_on_id":"bd-2dovu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pa8p","depends_on_id":"bd-2tb5i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pa8p","depends_on_id":"bd-4uecx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pa8p","depends_on_id":"bd-9l7cp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3pbe","title":"EPIC: Comprehensive Audit Log System","description":"## Overview
Implement an immutable, structured audit log that records all significant NTM actions, enabling debugging, compliance, and operational insights.

## Problem Statement
NTM lacks comprehensive activity tracking. Users can't:
- Debug what happened in a session hours ago
- Prove compliance for regulated environments
- Understand why something failed
- Track who/what made changes
- Reconstruct incident timelines

## Key Features
1. Immutable append-only log file per session
2. Structured JSON format for machine parsing
3. Human-readable summary export
4. Query commands: ntm audit search, ntm audit show
5. Retention policies with automatic cleanup
6. Integration with enterprise logging (syslog, CloudWatch)

## Log Entry Types
- command: CLI commands executed
- spawn: Agent creation events
- send: Prompts sent to agents
- response: Agent responses (summarized)
- error: Errors and warnings
- state_change: Agent state transitions
- config_change: Configuration modifications

## Log Format (JSONL)
{
  "timestamp": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "session_id": "myproject",
  "event_type": "send",
  "actor": "user",
  "target": "myproject__cc_1",
  "payload": {"prompt_preview": "Fix the auth bug...", "prompt_length": 500},
  "metadata": {"correlation_id": "abc123"}
}

## CLI Commands
- ntm audit show <session> - view session audit log
- ntm audit search <query> - search across all logs
- ntm audit export <session> --format=json|csv|html
- ntm audit tail - live follow of current session

## Success Criteria
- All significant actions logged with timestamps
- Logs are tamper-evident (checksums)
- Can reconstruct session timeline from log
- Query tools work efficiently
- Compliant with common audit requirements

## Files Likely Affected
- internal/audit/logger.go (new - audit logging)
- internal/audit/query.go (new - search/filter)
- internal/audit/export.go (new - export formats)
- internal/cli/audit.go (new - CLI commands)","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-3pnw7","title":"Subtask: Implement Agent-Specific Pattern Definitions","description":"## Subtask: Implement Agent-Specific Pattern Definitions

### Purpose
Define regex patterns and string matchers for each agent type. This is the "knowledge base" of how each agent expresses its state.

### File to Create
`internal/agent/patterns.go`

### Pattern Categories

For each agent type, we need patterns for:
1. **Rate Limit Detection** - Agent hit usage cap
2. **Context/Usage Indicators** - How much capacity remains
3. **Working Indicators** - Agent actively producing output
4. **Idle Indicators** - Agent waiting for input
5. **Error Indicators** - Something went wrong

### Claude Code (cc) Patterns

```go
package agent

import "regexp"

// Claude Code patterns
var (
    // Rate limit - agent cannot continue
    ccRateLimitPatterns = []string{
        "You.ve hit your limit",
        "Rate limit exceeded", 
        "Please wait",
        "try again later",
        "too many requests",
        "usage limit",
    }
    
    // Context warnings - running low
    ccContextWarnings = []string{
        "This conversation is getting long",
        "context limit",
        "running out of context",
        "conversation.*long",
    }
    
    // Working - actively producing (DO NOT INTERRUPT)
    ccWorkingPatterns = []string{
        "```",           // Code block delimiter
        "Writing to ",
        "Created ",
        "Modified ",
        "Deleted ",
        "Reading ",
        "Searching ",
        "Running ",
        "Executing ",
        "Installing ",
        "Thinking",
        "Processing",
        "Analyzing",
    }
    
    // Idle - waiting for input (safe to restart)
    ccIdlePatterns = []*regexp.Regexp{
        regexp.MustCompile(`>\s*$`),           // Prompt
        regexp.MustCompile(`Human:\s*$`),      // Conversation mode
        regexp.MustCompile(`waiting for input`),
    }
    
    // Header detection - confirms this is Claude Code
    ccHeaderPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`Opus \d+\.\d+.*Claude`)
)
```

### Codex CLI (cod) Patterns

```go
var (
    // CRITICAL: Codex shows explicit context percentage!
    // "47% context left · ? for shortcuts"
    codContextPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`(\d+)%\s*context\s*left`)
    
    // Token usage after response
    // "Token usage: total=219,582 input=206,150 ..."
    codTokenPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`Token usage:\s*total=(\d[\d,]*)`)
    
    // Rate limit
    codRateLimitPatterns = []string{
        "You.ve reached your usage limit",
        "Rate limit exceeded",
        "quota exceeded",
        "capacity",
    }
    
    // Working indicators
    codWorkingPatterns = []string{
        "```",
        "Editing ",
        "Creating ",
        "Writing ",
        "Running ",
        "$ ",  // Shell command output
    }
    
    // Idle
    codIdlePatterns = []*regexp.Regexp{
        regexp.MustCompile(`>\s*$`),
        regexp.MustCompile(`\?\s*for shortcuts`), // Prompt line
    }
    
    // Header detection
    codHeaderPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`codex|openai`)
)
```

### Gemini CLI (gmi) Patterns

```go
var (
    // Memory usage (less precise than Codex)
    // "gemini-3-pro-preview /model | 396.8 MB"
    gmiMemoryPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`(\d+\.?\d*)\s*MB`)
    
    // YOLO mode indicator
    gmiYoloPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`YOLO mode:\s*(ON|OFF)`)
    
    // Rate limit (less explicit - heuristic)
    gmiRateLimitPatterns = []string{
        "error",
        "failed",
        "quota",
        "limit reached",
        "try again",
    }
    
    // Working indicators
    gmiWorkingPatterns = []string{
        "```",
        "Creating ",
        "Writing ",
        "Executing ",
    }
    
    // GOTCHA: Shell mode triggered by "!"
    gmiShellModePattern = regexp.MustCompile(`^!\s*`)
    
    // Header detection
    gmiHeaderPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`gemini.*preview|gemini-\d`)
)
```

### Pattern Matching Helpers

```go
// matchAny returns true if text matches any pattern
func matchAny(text string, patterns []string) bool {
    textLower := strings.ToLower(text)
    for _, p := range patterns {
        if strings.Contains(textLower, strings.ToLower(p)) {
            return true
        }
    }
    return false
}

// matchAnyRegex returns true if text matches any regex
func matchAnyRegex(text string, patterns []*regexp.Regexp) bool {
    for _, p := range patterns {
        if p.MatchString(text) {
            return true
        }
    }
    return false
}

// extractFloat extracts first float from regex match
func extractFloat(pattern *regexp.Regexp, text string) *float64 {
    match := pattern.FindStringSubmatch(text)
    if len(match) < 2 {
        return nil
    }
    val, err := strconv.ParseFloat(match[1], 64)
    if err != nil {
        return nil
    }
    return &val
}
```

### Why These Specific Patterns?

1. **Rate limit patterns are broad** - False positives here are OK (we just wait). False negatives are costly (we might interrupt).

2. **Working patterns include code blocks** - The "```" delimiter is the most reliable indicator of active output.

3. **Codex context pattern is most valuable** - It is the ONLY agent that gives explicit percentage. We extract this precisely.

4. **Gemini patterns are heuristic** - Gemini is less explicit, so we use softer matching and lower confidence.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All patterns compile without regex errors
- [ ] Patterns are case-insensitive where appropriate
- [ ] Helper functions handle edge cases
- [ ] Patterns documented with examples","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-20T21:58:37.263265726Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T01:39:35.629175929Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T01:39:35.628338021Z","close_reason":"Implemented agent-specific patterns for cc, cod, gmi agents including rate limit, working, idle, error patterns, and helper functions for pattern matching. All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3pnw7","depends_on_id":"bd-120q6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pnw7","depends_on_id":"bd-2iw6e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
